scope.family: encoded-mouth stabilizer resources plus a coherent two-screen routing isometry
state_derived_data: logical mouth map from full encoded-block mutual information
screen success probabilities from induced screen channels
equal-area entropy analogue from the same channel-derived probabilities

core_claim: Full encoded-resource state data can infer the mouth map that low-order entropy misses; activating the inferred map restores exact transfer, while wrong-mouth and mouth-blind controls fail. Screen recovery and equal-area entropy/area ordering are computed from the same channel-derived success probabilities.

theorem_style_claims.claim: For the encoded-mouth stabilizer family, each left mouth has a unique full-block mutual-information maximum at its paired right encoded block.
Low-order physical entropy through code distance does not identify the mouth map, but the state-derived activation recovers the exact logical channel.
Screen success probabilities inferred from coherent routing channels determine both recovery ordering and the equal-bare-area entropy/area analogue transition.
An independent bare-area bias not encoded in the state/channel shifts the area crossing without shifting the recovery crossing.

bounded_exhaustive_evidence.scope: aligned and twisted two-mouth encoded resources

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 14 state-derived bridge dynamics certificate.
Regression check for the Goal 14 certificate.

diagnostic_hierarchy.coarse_entropy_mincut: insufficient for the encoded mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.low_order_physical_entropy_through_dista: insufficient for the encoded mouth map
diagnostic_hierarchy.full_encoded_block_mutual_information: infers the mouth map in the declared family
diagnostic_hierarchy.state_derived_activation: restores exact transfer in the declared family
diagnostic_hierarchy.mouth_blind_scrambling_control: fails to produce structured named-mouth transfer
diagnostic_hierarchy.channel_derived_screen_success: determines the finite recovery and equal-area transition

known_vs_new.known_derived: Standard stabilizer distance, encoded Bell resources, mutual information, Choi/Kraus teleportation checks, and erasure-channel recovery.
known_vs_new.new_programmatic_contribution: Packages a finite benchmark in which the mouth map and screen transition parameter are read from the state/channel data rather than supplied directly to the decoder.

limitations: This is an exact finite stabilizer/QEC benchmark. Full encoded-block mutual information is decoder-scale data, the equal-area analogue is not a gravitational area derivation, and the model is not continuum ER=EPR, de Sitter physics, dS/CFT, or chaotic many-body traversability.
next_frontier: Replace the product encoded-mouth resource plus coherent erasure router with one interacting finite circuit or tensor network whose coupling dynamics, observer algebra, and area-like transition are all derived from the same non-product construction.
